query: we have a termbase.csv from last month's terminology audit sitting in docs/glossary/ — can you turn it into a vocabulary section for our AGENTS.md so the codegen agents stop inventing synonyms for things
check my draft spec at openspec/changes/add-caching/spec.md against the approved terms in termbase.csv and tell me where the wording drifts
someone on the team keeps writing 'execution' instead of 'run' in our proposals. we have a canonical terms csv from an audit. can you lint the proposals/ dir for that kind of thing and suggest fixes
we just merged the scheduler implementation, scan the new code in src/scheduler for recurring domain terms that aren't in our termbase yet and propose candidates for the next revision
render our glossary csv into a compact preamble block i can paste at the top of proposal.md so reviewers see the binding vocabulary
our specs keep flip flopping between extend mode, extension mode and inherit mode for the same feature. we already have a termbase with the approved term — enforce it on specs/*.md
take the terminology audit output (termbase.csv, columns preferred_term / forbidden_variants) and make it into something claude will actually follow when it drafts tasks.md files
vocab drift check on my tasks file pls, termbase is in the repo root
before I cut tasks from this spec can you verify it only uses approved terminology? the term list is at data/terms.csv
what new domain terms did the payments refactor introduce that aren't in termbase.csv? give me counts and example locations, don't touch the termbase itself
audit our engineering docs for inconsistent terminology and build me a termbase and a report on which variants dominate
translate the terms in our glossary.csv into French and German for the localization team, keep the definitions column as is
write an openspec proposal and spec for the new caching layer, following the conventions in openspec/README.md
rename the variable extendMode to inheritMode across the whole codebase and update the tests
show me a stem and leaf diagram of which terms dominate across our wiki and where they conflict
proofread my spec.md for grammar, spelling and passive voice before I send it for review
terms.csv has duplicate rows with slightly different ids — dedupe it and merge the definition columns into one clean spreadsheet
add a definitions section to the README that explains what extend mode and merge policy mean for new contributors
run eslint on src/ and fix the naming-convention warnings it reports
summarize the terminology-audit report in examples/sample-reports/planets into three bullet points for a slide
